I met my wife when I first moved to Chattanooga in 1941. She was just 11 years old, and I was about 18. We met at Ridgedale Baptist Church, where I started substitute teaching and guiding the children in song, encouraging their participation and helping them learn the melodies and lyrics. After she graduated from high school, she went to Carson-Newman College, and we got married in October 1952. We just celebrated our 50th wedding anniversary in October.
